summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the pinned StableLib snapshot accepts an all-zero peer public key and derives the same X25519 session keys that an attacker can compute from public transcript data alone.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show that an all-zero peer public key yields an all-zero raw shared secret and matching victim/attacker send and receive keys on the default X25519 session helper path.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned StableLib at commit 0f153a6, built the required packages in Docker, and printed `EVOHUNT_X25519_ZERO_KEY_POC_OK` together with identical victim and attacker send/receive keys after the victim processed an all-zero peer public key.
verification_method: The containerized verifier constructs the malicious peer input, checks that the victim shared secret is all-zero, recomputes the attacker-side session keys from public transcript data, and exits successfully only if the victim and attacker send/receive keys match exactly. The host run script then requires the proof string to appear in the run log.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run log records an all-zero victim shared key.
The run log records identical victim and attacker send/receive keys.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the StableLib repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
